about:
### Hey, I'm Amin 👋

I've spent the last decade obsessed with one question: **how do you build software that scales — not just technically, but for the team maintaining it at 2am?**

I'm a Lead Backend Architect with deep roots in **Fintech and HealthTech**, where the stakes are real and downtime costs lives or money. I've designed trading engines that handle hundreds of thousands of orders, FHIR-compliant healthcare platforms across German medical providers, and distributed ERP systems that run factory floors in real-time.

But honestly? The part I'm proudest of isn't any of those systems. It's the engineers I've watched grow alongside them.

#### Leadership & Mentorship

I've had the privilege of leading and mentoring **30+ developers** across 5 companies. I genuinely believe a system is only as good as the team that maintains it. My approach is direct but patient — I'd rather spend an hour explaining the *why* behind an architectural decision than ship something nobody understands.

I've made my share of wrong calls, refactored things I was sure were perfect, and learned that **clean architecture is less about elegance and more about being kind to your future self**.

#### Technical Depth

My core stack is **Node.js + TypeScript**, with production experience across PostgreSQL, ArangoDB, MongoDB, and Redis. I've designed microservice networks, event-driven pipelines with RabbitMQ, real-time systems using WebSockets, and server-driven UI frameworks that push rendering control to the backend.

I started my career in C++ writing image-processing algorithms for remote sensing research — that mathematical grounding shapes how I think about data precision and performance to this day.

#### Available & Ready

Currently based in **Antalya, Turkey** with a transferable Turkish work permit. Happy to support **EU or North American** time zones. Open to full-time lead roles or senior consulting contracts — remote or on-site.
quote: I care less about the cleverness of the code and more about whether the next developer can understand it at 2am.
